sections.title: Security
sections.intro:
Strong random secrets will be generated automatically.

You do not need to enter JWT, session, database, or storage passwords — they are created for you in the background.

sections.title: Web server
sections.intro: These settings control how users reach Atlantisboard in their browser.

fields.label: Application port
fields.description: Port the app listens on (e.g. 3000). Open this port in your firewall or point your reverse proxy here.

fields.label: Bind address
fields.description: Use 0.0.0.0 to accept connections on all network interfaces.

fields.label: Public site URL
fields.description: The URL people type in the browser (e.g. https://boards.example.com). Use http://localhost:3000 for local testing.

fields.label: Allowed browser origins
fields.description: Usually the same as your public site URL. Separate multiple origins with commas.

fields.label: Reverse proxy hops
fields.description: Set to 1 when Nginx or Caddy sits in front of the app. Use 0 if there is no reverse proxy.

sections.title: Google sign-in (optional)
sections.intro:
To enable Sign in with Google, create OAuth credentials in Google Cloud Console.

Leave every field blank to skip Google sign-in — you can add it later in Admin settings.

fields.label: Google OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud → APIs & Services → Credentials → OAuth 2.0 Client IDs.

fields.label: Google OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: The secret shown next to your OAuth client in Google Cloud.

fields.label: Google OAuth browser origin
fields.description: Must match an authorized redirect origin in Google Cloud (e.g. https://boards.example.com).

sections.title: MongoDB database
sections.intro: Connect to your existing MongoDB server. The URI must include replicaSet= for real-time collaboration.

fields.label: MongoDB connection string
fields.description: Example: mongodb://user:pass@db.example.com:27017/kanboard?replicaSet=rs0

fields.label: Database name
fields.description: MongoDB database name for Atlantisboard data.

sections.title: Redis cache
sections.intro: Redis stores sessions and rate-limit counters.

fields.label: Redis hostname
fields.description: Hostname or IP of your Redis server.

fields.label: Redis port
fields.description: Usually 6379.

fields.label: Redis password
fields.description: Must match the password configured on your Redis server.

sections.title: File storage (MinIO / S3)
sections.intro:
MinIO (or S3-compatible storage) holds attachments, backups, and branding files.

Access credentials were generated automatically in the Security step.

fields.label: Storage hostname
fields.description: Hostname only — not a full URL (e.g. minio.example.com or localhost).

fields.label: Storage port
fields.description: S3 API port (usually 9000).

fields.label: Use TLS for storage
fields.description: Enter true if storage uses HTTPS/TLS, otherwise false.

sections.title: Background jobs & backups
sections.intro: Scheduled backups and background tasks can run in a separate worker process.

fields.label: Run background jobs in main app
fields.description: Enter false to use a separate worker service (recommended). Enter true for a single process.

fields.label: Backup folder
fields.description: Absolute path where admin backup ZIP files are stored.

sections.title: Docker storage
sections.intro:
Docker will store database and file data in named volumes.

MongoDB and MinIO credentials were generated automatically.

Reinstalling with new secrets requires removing old volumes (see install/docker/reset-docker-data.sh).

fields.label: MongoDB admin username
fields.description: Internal MongoDB root user (auto-generated password).

fields.label: MongoDB app username
fields.description: Application database user (auto-generated password).

fields.label: Database name
fields.description: MongoDB database name.
